# Gnosis Source

Parent: [Gnosis README](../README.md)

GG developer docs:
[developer guide](../docs/GG_DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md),
[modules and extensions](../docs/GG_MODULES_AND_EXTENSIONS.md),
[surface map](../docs/GG_SURFACE_MAP.md).

This directory contains the Gnosis compiler, runtime, and execution tooling.

## Getting Started

- **What:** the TypeScript source map for Gnosis compiler, runtime, reducers, contracts, and exported package subpaths.
- **Why:** it tells you which module owns which behavior before you import or edit a public surface.
- **How:** search this README for the export or concept you need, then open the named module and its tests.
- **Next:** use this as the implementation index after reading the top-level README or getting-started guide.

## Top-Level Modules

- `index.ts`: CLI/runtime orchestration entrypoint (kept for CLI workflows), including `build`/`verify`, `--verbose` REPL banner mode, `--steering-mode <off|report|suggest|apply>`, and optional `--execution-auth-json` / `--execution-auth-file` boundary-walk UCAN envelopes.
- `apm.ts`: optional OTEL/APM bootstrap for CLI/runtime surfaces, including Datadog OTLP env resolution through `@a0n/telemetry` and reusable span helpers.
- `telemetry-module.d.ts`: local type shim for the shared `@a0n/telemetry`
  package so Node16 `rootDir`-strict typecheck stays scoped to `src/` while
  still emitting runtime imports against the shared package.
- CLI easter eggs: `crank` = Wallace check, `stank` = Buley check.
- `lib.ts`: Package main entry used by `@a0n/gnosis` imports; the package root now points back at this library barrel so runtime consumers do not accidentally execute the CLI-only `index.ts` graph.
- `foil-edge-gate.ts`: default fail-closed FOIL edge admission gate exported
  as `@a0n/gnosis/foil-edge-gate`. It accepts only static Lean prover
  zero-deficit certificates whose verifier cost stays within the constant
  bound, and rejects WebGPU/bare-metal topology execution before dispatch when
  the certificate leaks deficit, leaves branch entropy unresolved, or violates
  the void bound. The admission decision is the canonical ternary
  `Verdict` (`@a0n/bitwise/triton-verdict`): clean → `Accept`, forged →
  `Decline`, and over-budget-but-well-formed (`verificationCost > constantBound`,
  latent / recheck-later) → `Abstain` — the middle the old `accepted` boolean
  squashed into a hard reject. Recovering it avoids a full re-prove on every
  latent proof; `__tests__/foil-race-speed.bench.test.ts` measures the payoff
  (up to ~34x lower admission cost at 20% latent / prove=200; ties when there
  are no latent proofs). The `Abstain` path is theorem-backed by
  `gnosis-math` `Gnosis.TritonForkRaceFold` (`fast_veto` /
  `straggler_defers_not_kills` / `monotone_commit`).
- `randomness.ts`: Randomness core — Lean trace anchors (`GNOSIS_RANDOMNESS_LEAN_MODULES`), simulation-only stance and disclaimer string, re-export of nested Lacey PRNG (`@a0n/gnosis/randomness` subpath). CSPRNG security is formalized in Lean, not asserted by this runtime.
- `probability-certificate.ts`: Runtime mirror of
  `Gnosis.FiniteProbabilityCore.RuntimeCertificate`, exposing safe-integer
  process, kernel, cover, completed-interface, and trace certificates with
  explicit mass balance, residual coverage, additive runtime traces, and
  routing badges. `observeProbabilityRuntime()` turns certificates into active
  decisions (`continue`, `cover`, `shrink-horizon`, or `human-escalation`).
  `GnosisEngine` now attaches both a process certificate and a trace certificate
  to `executeWithResult()` so interpreted topology runs report visible execution
  mass, residual debt, branch outcomes, and cache/corridor evidence.
- `gnosis-time/`: discrete aeon dial (`@a0n/gnosis/gnosis-time`) aligned with Lean **`Gnosis.GnosisTimeClock`** — see [gnosis-time/README.md](./gnosis-time/README.md).
- `protocol69.ts`: canonical Monster/FOIL RF projection contract exported as
  `@a0n/gnosis/protocol69` and through the root barrel. It names the numeric
  projection `66 xor 7 = 69`, reserves the word-form label `sixty9`, and keeps
  runtime integer projection separate from the Lean Fano parity bridge in
  `Gnosis.RemoteCacheKeyTeleportation`. It also exposes the iota program
  teleport path for in-room Pluto SDR app handoff: binary iota source inflates
  to `.gg`, Betty validates the executable topology, and the run receipt returns
  a deterministic protocol69 compute-cache key whose material includes the Lean
  module, source node count, inflated `.gg` node/edge counts, `7` local operand,
  `69` projection, and program checksum.
- `protocol69-r1-witness.ts`: canonical R1 BTLE witness report contract
  exported as `@a0n/gnosis/protocol69-r1-witness`. It wraps the protocol69
  projection envelope with device/channel/RSSI/cadence evidence so aeon-shell
  can submit channelized entropy witnesses to FOIL without claiming executable
  compute or raw IQ access.
- `iota-trees.ts`: FOIL/iota source-topology bridge exported as
  `@a0n/gnosis/iota-trees`. It mirrors the Lean `Gnosis.IotaTrees` surface by
  rendering unlabeled binary iota source trees into `.gg` nodes and left/right
  edges, then reconstructing the tree from Betty `GraphAST` only when the
  topology is bijective: every `IotaApp` has exactly one left child and one
  right child, leaves have no required children, and cycles are rejected.
- `runtime/ambient-scheduler.ts`: Ambient request/runtime entropy folded into memory-bounded work schedules for Worker, browser, gnode, polyglot, and native-adjacent execution surfaces. It changes traversal/admission order without duplicating payloads or increasing WASM memory.
- `runtime/ambient-substrate.ts`: Substrate adapter for `polyglot`, `gnode`, `gnexec`, `monster`, and `moonshine`, with a `GNOSIS_AMBIENT_*` env handoff for CLI/Rust-backed surfaces and the same memory-first admission contract.
- `topological-xor-certificate.ts`: Finite `F₂` boundary certificate surface (`@a0n/gnosis/topological-xor-certificate`) that folds runtime traces, model-boundary scans, agent task graphs, and void-boundary counts by XOR parity. Balanced measured inputs certify as `measured`; balanced projected inputs remain `projected`; unresolved odd classes produce `failed` certificates with explicit defect rank and boundary keys. The projection-review layer attaches later measured evidence and returns `promoted-after-measurement`, `demoted-after-measurement`, or `under-investigation` without rewriting the historical projection. The skip-rule API turns balanced certificates into explicit `skip-reconstruction` decisions, keeps projected closure on `measure-before-skip` unless the caller accepts `skip-with-projection-debt`, sends odd classes to `reconstruct-boundary`, and batches decisions into skip/measure/reconstruct plans for runtime consumers. Lean anchors include `InftyOperads.K4_boundary_squared_zero`, `InftyOperads.K4_H1_zero`, `OneCobFrobenius.frobenius_identity`, `BraidCatalogFinal.braid_catalog_final_witness`, `FrfInherentGestalt.xorMerge_append`, `FrfWitnessTowerTruncation.xor_merge_take_drop_split`, and `CertificationDemotion.projection_within_family_can_fail_at_scale`.
- `physics-sandbox.ts`: Deterministic numeric integrators (`@a0n/gnosis/physics-sandbox`); Euler + RK2 midpoint; Lean anchors (`GNOSIS_PHYSICS_SANDBOX_LEAN_MODULES`), `SHANNON_FAIR_BINARY_NATS_JS`, `integrateLinearAutonomous` / `integrateLinearAutonomousRk2` for `x' = a x` demos — see `docs/PHYSICS_SANDBOX.md`.
- `seismic-stress-toy.ts`: Discrete-time load/threshold/slip toy for a single asperity, parallel patches, two-node stress relaxation (`relaxTwoPatch`, `κ ∈ [0,1]` aligned with Lean), optional θ-aging with linear μ\*(θ), optional θ reset on slip, coupled stepping (`stepCoupledTwoPatch`, `simulateCoupledTwoPatch`, `stepCoupledTwoPatchRateState`, `simulateCoupledTwoPatchRateState`) (`@a0n/gnosis/seismic-stress-toy`); not continuum seismology — see [docs/SEISMIC_STRESS_TOY.md](../docs/SEISMIC_STRESS_TOY.md), `SeismicStressToySketch` / `SeismicRelaxRealSketch` in `lean/Lean/PlanetaryHomologySandbox/`. Demo: `node ./bin/gnode.js run src/seismic-stress-toy-demo.ts --export main`.
- `math-sandbox.ts`: Lean trace anchors for the `Init`-first math hub (`@a0n/gnosis/math-sandbox`); `GnosisMathPrelude` / `Hyperoperations` module ids; integration candidates (`GreekLogicCanon`, `ZeckendorfFST` peel); optional `kraftSumFloat` and `fibZWeights` (Zeckendorf/Fibonacci tower demos, not proof-checked in TS) — see `docs/MATH_SANDBOX.md` and `scripts/validate-init-only-import-closure.mjs`.
- `repl.js`: source shim that preserves the CLI's `./repl.js` dynamic import while delegating the actual Ink REPL implementation to `repl.tsx` under direct `gnode` source execution.
- `formal-ledger.ts`: Canonical math-derived contract kernel for non-technical platform services, exposing `deriveFormalLedgerBundle(...)`, fixed `fork/race/fold/vent/interfere` primitives, service declarations, the exact statistical mechanics accounting certificate, braid-packet certificate, terminal 43 gnosis-uring interfere-port certificate, pressure-weighted interference stride certificate, gnostic lens geometry certificate, gnostic metrology profile, diagnostics, narratives, recommendations, native probability-analysis reduction, native Bazaar/Neutral negotiation-profile reduction, native communication-field reduction, native metacognition/recovery reductions for Halogram-style C0-C3 and stress-response surfaces, native justice reductions for evidence/adjudication state, native governance reductions for Aeon Voting tallies and process pressure, native trust reduction for authority/manipulation surfaces, native behavior reduction for deterministic loop analyses, and visualization payloads for the `voidWalking`, `negotiation`, `community`, `metacognition`, `recovery`, and `probability` families.
- `dark-shroud.ts`: deterministic reducer for the deployable dark shroud itself, mapping port/trust/risk observations into mirrored/rate-limited/quarantined route decisions plus a canonical `x-ranger` boundary manifest. It is theorem-informed by the existing mesh/Buleyean Lean floor, but it is not yet a new Lean mechanization of port-route policy synthesis.
- `x-ranger-boundary.ts`: deterministic boundary contract for the future `open-source/x-ranger` shell/`gnosis-ranger` manager split, including typed mesh-policy manifests, inspection-floor declarations, digest generation, and self-audit helpers so the runtime side can consume one canonical Gnosis-owned schema instead of inventing an ad hoc deployment format.
- `twin-psyche-formalization.ts`: Canonical twin/psyche theorem catalog and deterministic reducer for the expanded identity, consciousness, knot-thermodynamic conflict, void-walking/deceptacon, emotion/personality, vent/exhaustion, and master-theorem surfaces. The catalog now exposes 105 explicit theorem ids plus exact Lean trace anchors for the newly promoted `PsycheGrind.lean` entries, then derives spotlight theorems, anti-thesis controls, richer diagnostics, and a supporting formal-ledger bundle for public twins, directories, simulations, relationship surfaces, and shell digests.
- `law.ts`: GG/AST adapter layer for the local Gnosis universe kernel, turning source surfaces into `LawProgram`s, producing universe certificates/admission reports, and attaching runtime metadata to law snapshots.
- `cli.ts`: CLI entrypoint used by `bin/gnosis.js`.
- `analysis.ts`: Formal complexity/steering analysis for `.gg` topologies, including canonical `wallaceNumber` frontier diagnostics with `wally`/`frontierDeficit` aliases, the FailureTrilemma/Composition/Universality collapse-cost surface (`totalVentCost`, `totalRepairDebt`, `paidStageCount`, `collapseCostFloor`, `freeCollapsePrefixRisk`), boundary-walk bias / lean-in classification, `twokeys` EDA summaries, separate microCharley wall/CPU time telemetry, and the unified `UniverseAdmissionReport` that merges correctness, capability validation, and law verdict into one report-only surface.
- `eyetracking.ts`: Gnosis-native eye-tracking corpus and failure kernel, including finite calibration ledgers, sharp zone admission, void-boundary failure accumulation, Merkle vent logs, QDoc persistence, replay-ready rejection export, failure-pressure summaries, canonical ETaaS dataset-slice/training-manifest builders, and native Buleyean corpus/job/delta contracts for downstream training and control-plane consumers.
- `avatar.ts`: compact avatar kernel for the Aeon hologram stack, including VRM extension interpretation, humanoid bone/morph manifest compilation, viseme/expression mixing, look-at solving, and deterministic pose evaluation for `aeon-avatar-3d`.
- `runtime/agent-swarm-contract.ts`: protected-branch multi-agent change adjudication kernel, including bounded task ownership, path/tool admissibility, verification custody, reviewer quorum, overlap checks, tamper-evident merge certificates, and self-audit helpers consumed by MCP and higher-trust merge surfaces.
- `runtime/worker-runtime.ts`: web/Cloudflare Worker GG hot path. Loads a compiled `GraphAST`, resolves handlers once, optionally feeds `FORK/RACE/FOLD/VENT` control edges through the Rust/WASM native frame runtime at load, and executes repeated requests through the compiled topology plan. The current `FORK -> FOLD` shootout measured the async compiled lane at `242.02x` over per-request `GnosisEngine` interpretation, and the sync-safe Worker built-in lane at `880.64x` (`22,027.318ms` vs `25.013ms` for 10,000 executions, two native control edges processed at load). Use this for stable web artifacts; keep `GnosisEngine` for full audited interpretation.
- `untangling-strategy.ts`: theorem-backed software-knot remediation guidance, translating staged expansion, composite-knot monotonicity, and hyperbolic minimum results into practical `hold` / `direct-simplify` / `expand-first` / `staged-expand-first` / `minimize-within-class` recommendations for downstream scanners and copilots.
- `void-dimensions.ts`: shared lower-bound accounting for cross-file, temporal, and data-plane invisible crossings, translating the `VoidDimensions.lean` exact-gap theorems into reusable scanner/runtime assessments without overstating what local static evidence can prove.
- `flow-obstruction.ts`: theorem-backed flow-obstruction kernel for shared scanner/runtime diagnostics, exporting the six obstruction kinds plus composition, makespan overhead, and performance-gap helpers from `FlowObstruction.lean`.
- `triangulation-dimensional-coercion.ts`: runtime mirror for cross-dimensional triangulation/sphere coercion (`2D ↔ 3D ↔ nD`), including Euler-2 sphere witnesses, coercion-matrix generation, explicit unknot-debt/drop metrics for dimensional lifts, bidirectional roundtrip checks, and reduction-input adapters consumed by `reduction-projection-scan.ts`.
- `decomposition-topology.ts`: runtime mirror of the bounded decomposition kernel, exposing finite `decompose(...)` arithmetic, featureless-state checks, and executable mirrors of the non-increasing / full-decomposition / residue / idempotence boundaries from `DecompositionTopology.lean`.
- `transformation-family-formalization.ts`: deterministic reducer for the bounded transformation-family kernel, classifying normalized examples as projection, reinforcement, decomposition, or explicit unclassified/restoration-required cases, emitting stable theorem ids for the family surface, exposing a fail-closed reinforcement-attempt bridge that emits actual signals only for successful attempts while keeping featureless/overfill failures explicit as blocked diagnostics, and exposing a decomposition-attempt bridge that turns bounded structural reductions into honest family signals and no-op/full-collapse diagnostics.
- `juggling-orbit-formalization.ts`: deterministic orbit-first reducer for algorithmic juggling exploration, including beat/hand conservation checks, state-transition orbit decomposition, asymmetric multiplex witnesses, and machine-only candidate boundaries.
- `certified-allocation-kernel.ts`: deterministic partial-observation safety kernel adjacent to the juggling orbit reducer, mirroring the aeon-eyetracker calibration and sharp-admission surface into a fail-closed allocation gate for dynamic resource allocation under uncertainty and downstream consumers such as `packages/forkjoin-hft`.
- `juggling-chaos-orchestration.ts`: deterministic orchestration surface for AI-style juggling composition, combining corpus-seeded generation, pluggable proposer modes (`markov`, `corpus-remix`, `hybrid`), chaos-style elitist evolution, gait-coupling scoring, and MPC-proxy feasibility metrics (horizon/non-convexity/dimensionality/uncertainty/warm-start gain) on top of the orbit formalization reducer.
- `pattern-horizon-compiler.ts`: deterministic theorem-backed kinetic planning kernel that now splits frontier compilation from admission, turning direct or composed juggling patterns into beat-indexed admissible windows, explicit frontier slots, fail-closed observation authority, and a selected action plus commit/hedge/escalation policy.
- `reynolds-bft.ts`: Pure Reynolds/BFT fold classifier surface exported at `@a0n/gnosis/reynolds-bft`, including `idleStages()`, `quorumSafeFold()`, `majoritySafeFold()`, and `classifyFoldRegime()` for theorem-backed `mergeAll` / `quorumFold` / `syncRequired` decisions without pulling the root barrel.
- `behavioral-taxonomy.ts`: builds a Gnosis topology from the open-source behavioral-loop corpus, infers prerequisite and same-origin couplings, measures the resulting buley number for the full dataset or filtered subsets, emits per-loop executable `.gg` topologies plus `.tla`/`.cfg` artifacts, and can execute single-loop behavior topologies through the runtime.
- `cross-domain.ts`: generic code/natural/GG adapter kernel, including explicit source/target descriptors, semantic facets and obligations, preservation validation, and the shared cross-compilation entrypoint used by `gnode`.
- `ts-bridge.ts`: strict TypeScript-to-GG bridge for orchestration entrypoints, lowering linear statements plus `Promise.all` fork/join regions into executable Gnosis topologies, explicit schedule waves, runtime handler bindings, and proof-backed braid metadata directives including the closed-loop runtime checksum that `gnode` can audit today and `x-gnosis` can host natively later, while lazily resolving `createRequire()` so Worker imports stay fail-closed instead of crashing on startup and keeping `type`-only imports out of the generated runtime binding module.
- `ts-host.ts`: repo-owned TypeScript host barrel that re-exports the bridge/runtime-module helpers without forcing callers onto raw compiler-entry files.
- `ts-check.ts`: unified TypeScript formal check surface that now merges TS-to-GG complexity/capability analysis with Rust polyglot diagnostics, theorem anchors, and ghost-mass leak debt accounting, then emits one diagnostic/SARIF stream for CLI, ESLint, editor, and scanner consumers.
- `ts-graph.ts`: repo-owned TypeScript call-graph barrel for compiler-adjacent graph analysis consumers.
- `polyglot-bridge.ts`: TypeScript bridge over the Rust polyglot binary, now carrying per-function diagnostics and `ghost_mass_ledger` payloads from the GG compiler into the `ts-check` and orchestration surfaces.
- `gnode-execution.ts`: Gnosis-owned precompiled artifact host for `gnode` bridge outputs, including artifact registration, `/.aeon/gnode/*` control-route handling, exact-request runtime corridor caching, and cannon versus single-thread scheduling that `x-gnosis` now reuses as a compatibility shell.
- `wasm-execution.ts`: Gnosis-owned `/.aeon/wasm/*` control surface for registered WASM guests, with status/register/execute routes that mirror the Gnosis runtime contract downstream hosts expose while preserving admitted request-scoped execution auth from forwarded Aeon/UCAN headers; optional topology admission exposes a host-proven runtime tuple and salts declared-pure ESC cache keys, while optional `escTier` plus request `esc` enables edge-side compute teleport when no execution auth is present.
- `wasm-execution-bridge.ts`: Gnosis-owned WASM guest bridge, including `WasmBridge*` handler registration, scalar/JSON ABI execution, relative asset resolution, and direct `executeWasmWithGnosis()` entrypoints for hosts like `x-gnosis`, with host-supplied execution-auth flowing into the engine instead of being trapped in the transport shell.
- `betti-wasm-manifest.ts`: `betti-wasm/1` artifact manifest contract for Betti/polyglot-emitted WASM modules — fail-closed validator, `toRegisterWasmArtifactRequest` spread into the WASM surface, and `gnosis-betti-manifest` custom-section parsing (pure section walking, no runtime).
- `betti-wasm-store.ts`: Betti WASM artifact registry store — D1 `betti_wasm_artifacts` upsert/load (sibling of `polyglot_fragments`, keyed by explicit emitter artifactId with a `fragmentKey` secondary column), R2 `betti/v1/<artifactId>.{wasm,manifest.json}` object pairs, sha256-over-`bytes[..byteLength]` integrity verification on both store and load, and a deterministic local file-registry mode for dev/CLI flows without bindings. Wave D3 adds nullable `owner_did`/`price_cents` columns (guarded `ADD COLUMN` migration in `ensureBettiWasmArtifactsTable`; pre-existing rows read back NULL) so cache-served replays can credit artifact owners.
- `betti-wasm-codegen.ts`: worker-side CompiledWasm artifact map codegen — scans emitted artifact pairs, verifies integrity, copies modules, and generates a deterministic `betti-artifacts.ts` (`bettiModules` Map + `bettiManifests`) plus a `*.wasm` ambient declaration for Workers deploy-time module imports.
- `betti-wasm-cli.ts`: `gnode wasm-store` / `gnode wasm-codegen` verbs — shells the Betti emitter (`--emit-wasm-out` module + manifest sidecar pair) into the local file registry and drives the artifact-map codegen; real D1/R2 persistence stays binding-injected via `betti-wasm-store.ts` (no local D1 shim is pretended). `wasm-store` accepts `--owner-did` / `--price-cents` (Wave D3) to record artifact ownership and listing price on the registry entry.
- `tauri-edge.ts`: canonical `tauri-edge` artifact, fragment, D1-index, UCAN, and ZK manifest contract shared by Forge publish and `x-gnosis` reconstruction.
- `tauri-edge-runtime.ts`: semantic Tauri host ABI on top of the Gnosis WASM lane, including `invoke`, reactor policy generation, runtime-binding admission, and protected-path ZK enforcement for `tauri-edge` guests.
- `runtime/subprocess-invoker.ts`: shared polyglot subprocess ABI, now including Node router, Go router, Python service, Ruby service, Java service, and standard script-service compatibility request envelopes that let hosts execute captured framework handlers in place or fall back to a plain per-request source-program contract when Ditto only knows the language. The Node router lane now serves Express, Hono, Koa, and Fastify, the Go router lane serves Gin, Chi, Fiber, and Echo, the Python service lane serves both Flask and FastAPI sources, and the script-service lane covers JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, Python, Ruby, Java, Rust, C, C++, C#, PHP, Bash, Scala, Kotlin, Swift, Haskell, OCaml, Lua, Elixir, and Zig sources through the shared `XGNOSIS_HTTP_*` envelope.
- `native-host-bridge.ts`: long-lived stdio bridge that keeps the Gnosis `gnode` and WASM host surfaces resident for native listeners such as `gnosis-uring`, now carrying forwarded auth headers and host-minted reactor-handle grants into the resident execution surfaces instead of discarding them at the bridge boundary.
- `native-execution-auth.ts`: normalized `X-Aeon-*` / `X-Ucan-Capabilities` header intake for native guest execution, resolving forwarded host headers plus explicit overrides into canonical `executionAuth` contexts, verifying host-minted request/response reactor grants before admission, rejecting replayed or scope-drifted envelopes, and reporting concise auth summaries back to native control clients.
- `certified-app.ts`: QDoc-first certified app envelope surface that binds universe admission, compiled artifact identity, capability/runtime requirements, and append-only Forge/AeonPID attestations into one canonical record with deterministic JSON mirrors for boundary consumers.
- `ts-call-graph.ts`: TypeScript call-graph extraction and GG topology synthesis, now using the same lazy Node require guard for Worker-safe imports.
- `ufcs.ts`: source-level lowering for narrow single-receiver call syntax like `func(value)` and `value.func()`, plus the shared normalization pass that expands GG structured primitives before compiler/runtime analysis.
- `structured-primitives.ts`: GG macro lowerer for first-class structured graph forms such as `WallingtonRotation`, `WorthingtonWhip`, `StructuredMoA`, and `HeteroMoAFabric`, expanding compact primitive declarations into the concrete chunked-rotation, shard-collapse, nested sparse-MoA, and backend-diverse mirrored-lane topologies they denote, including the explicit MiddleOut request-compression corridor/trace/vent spine on lowered `StructuredMoA` collapse boundaries and the cannon/helix paired-layer collapse shape for lowered Hetero MoA fabrics.
- `gnarly-parser.ts`: `.gnarly` parser for the multilingual GG-family source format, preserving GG node/edge syntax while extracting optional `gnarly { ... }` metadata and `impl <node> in <language> { ... }` implementation blocks.
- `gnarly-compiler.ts`: `.gnarly` compiler/analyzer that lowers documents into Betty-compatible GG, polyglot execution manifests, multi-language manifests, generated implementation bundles, and non-blocking fastest-language hints backed by the Gnosis polyglot registry.
- `aeon-flux-site-witness.ts`: compiler-side witness synthesis for emitted Aeon Flux sites, currently recovering the `WallingtonRotation` chunk/path site surface into typed `pathCount` / `streamCount` / `Δβ` adequacy witnesses keyed to the in-tree Lean theorem family.
- `steering-trace.ts`: `QDoc`/Aeon-relay-backed steering trace stream plus indexed, bounded `twokeys` cohort summaries for empirical Wallace-vs-microCharley time analysis across runs and nodes, including aggregate failure-trajectory payment fields (`totalVentCost`, `totalRepairDebt`, `paidStageCount`, `collapseCostFloor`, `freeCollapsePrefixRisk`) with loud alerting for failures, invalid CRDT events, relay disconnects, and UCAN-denied boundary-walk surfaces.
- `neural-compat.ts`: `.gg`-native neural runtime exposing [`@affectively/neural`](https://github.com/forkjoin-ai/neural/)'s `NeuralEngine` interface, including `GPUEngine`, `WebGPUEngine`, `WebNNEngine`, canonical topic-domain module constants, neural graph repositories/types, and a `runHeteroMoAFabric()` bridge that launches available CPU/WebGPU/WebNN/WASM/env command backends through the learned cross-layer hetero-fabric meta-race runtime.
- `benchmarks/`: learned benchmark kernels for the Chapter 17 fold-boundary evidence package, including the original cancellation learner, the one-path negative controls, the fine-grained near-control zoom, the continuous regime sweep, the adversarial-control suite, the harder mini-MoE routing learner, the mirrored hetero-fabric runtime benchmark with both synthetic and discovered-backend live modes plus Cloud Run provenance and community-memory launch scheduling, and the MoA sweep/ablation evidence layer that now reuses unchanged dense baselines across sparse-routing ablations.

## Mesh-policy subpath exports

Four `@a0n/gnosis/*` subpaths compose the mesh-policy stack:

- [`mesh-policy`](./mesh-policy.ts) -- formal SSOT. TS mirror of the ten
  zero-sorry Lean modules under `gnosis/lean/Lean/ForkRaceFoldTheorems/`. Use
  this for structural primitives (`createMesh`, `createOverlay`, `createHoneyPit`,
  `linkAdmitted`, closed-architecture braid certificates, the natural
  10/12/17/22 fork-race-fold-vent port map, infinite cyclic scheduler,
  named vent-to-next-fork boundary, and 1560-mass braid packet certificate,
  proof-scoped extended-aeon 43 transition plus 55 closure certificate,
  terminal/off-cycle `BraidPortAction.interfere` mapping for native gnosis-uring handoff,
  pressure-weighted interference stride lineage, gnostic-lens geometry, and
  closed-loop runtime metadata checksum `6439`,
  the Wankel 12-cell/264-pulse runtime certificate, fast-path equivalence gate,
  single-pair measured-cost dominance gate, aggregate measured-cost dominance gate,
  and robust-estimator gate,
  statistical mechanics accounting over the exact 10/30/90 saturation trace,
  gnostic metrology, and calibrated fluidic-pressure bridge,
  routing-admissibility certificates, finite indexed pressure-selector theorem lineage with generic
  minimum-correctness, membership-preservation, stable equal-pressure
  first-candidate tie behavior for raw and budgeted selectors, runtime
  candidate-order alignment for exact and generic budgeted ties, input-index
  zero candidate preservation, over-budget tied rejection, combined selector
  certificate anchors, and pressure-budget monotonicity theorem lineage).
- [`mesh-policy-edge`](./mesh-policy-edge.ts) -- thin-client runtime. Pure
  Web-Fetch-API implementation of `wrapWithMeshPolicy`, `checkRequest`,
  `describeMeshPolicyPosture`, `auditPolicy`, `buildLinkFromBinding`,
  `auditLinkedServices`. Node-free. Target: Cloudflare Workers, Deno, Bun,
  Node 16+, browser.
- [`mesh-policy-compile`](./mesh-policy-compile.ts) -- build-time compiler.
  Emits a `.bw` binary bundle from a `MeshPolicyConfig`. Deterministic; produces
  a content-addressed `bundleFingerprint` (sha256) for idempotent deploys.
  Grounded by the `MeshPolicyCompileCorrectness.lean` round-trip theorem.
- [`mesh-policy-edge-compiled`](./mesh-policy-edge-compiled.ts) -- consumer.
  Deserializes `.bw` bundles back into the same `wrapWithMeshPolicy` contract.
  Used by generated loader modules in deploy targets.

## Subdirectories

- [auth](./auth): Native UCAN/ZK/custodial runtime integration, topology-local auth bootstrap, HALT attestation verification, host-owned `aeon_reactor_v1` policy hashing plus TEE/runtime-handle attestation helpers, execution-envelope authorization helpers, browser-safe binary encoding helpers, and steering/boundary-walk capability contracts.
- [benchmarks](./benchmarks/README.md): seeded benchmark helpers for parameter-matched `.gg` topologies, including the cancellation learner, the one-path negative controls, the near-control zoom, the regime sweep, the adversarial controls, the mini-MoE routing learner, the Aeon-framed transformer battery, the mirrored hetero-fabric runtime benchmark with synthetic/live launch-schedule output and Cloud Run env stamping, the direct rotated MoA-vs-regular transformer shootout, and the paper-oriented sweep/ablation evidence layer used by the Chapter 17 companion artifacts.
- [betty](./betty/README.md): Betty bootstrap compiler, parser, closed-variant exhaustiveness checks, UFCS lowering, thermodynamic stability auditing, certified-kernel Lean proof artifact generation, theorem-backed optimization pass manager (Reynolds fold-regime propagation via `THM-REYNOLDS-BFT-REGIME`, coarsening via `THM-RECURSIVE-COARSENING-SYNTHESIS`, codec racing via `THM-TOPO-RACE-SUBSUMPTION`, warmup efficiency via `THM-WARMUP-CONTROLLER`), fiber-annotated recursive coarsening synthesis, direct spectral-only certification for acyclic no-drift lowered topologies such as sink-wrapped `StructuredMoA`, deterministic/measurable queue proof emission over `queueSupportKernel`, metric-level Lévy-Prokhorov exact-convergence, geometric-decay, abstract geometric-ergodicity queue emission, a real-observable `continuousHarris` witness bridge over the emitted queue kernel with bounded affine drift gaps and generated observable/drift/Harris theorem families, the shared coupled-kernel handoff theorem for bounded inter-app arrival pressure, stochastic witness-kernel quantitative Harris certificates over `queueWitnessKernel`, and quantum bridge.
- [capabilities](./capabilities/README.md): Runtime capability profiles, effect-contract summaries, and target validation (`workers`, `node`, `bun`, `gnode`, `wasm-browser`, `wasi`, `tauri-edge`).
- [esc](./esc/README.md): Edge-side compute (ESC) — content-addressed cache keys over packed-knot slices and canonical inputs, `EscMemoryTier`, and WASM execution teleport hooks (`@a0n/gnosis/esc`).
- [crdt](./crdt/README.md): topology-native CRDT primitives including `QDoc` and the corridor/superposition primitive `QCorridor`, plus the generic Aeon relay adapter, DashRelay compatibility shim, exported typed map-change event contracts, and the `.qdoc` record substrate now used by `gnode`'s artifact/head cache with optional relay federation.
- [mod](./mod/README.md): `gnosis mod` package manager commands plus native `.mgg` module parsing, local vendored resolution, Forge-registry fallback for declared bare specifiers, merged-source loading, and dependency/source/capability checks against module-level universe certificate digests.
- [runtime](./runtime): Runtime registry, interpreter engine, `QDoc`-backed core cache/corridor state, ambient fail-closed law admission, per-execution `LawState` snapshots, direct law/physics/dimension/quantum built-ins, an internal structured-concurrency core for the Gnosis multiprocessing analogue, native `Result`/`Option`/`Variant`/`Destructure`/`Delay` handlers, case-aware branch routing, path-aware and tuple-aware destructuring, persistent execution-auth propagation, structured concurrency semantics for `RACE`/`FOLD`, explicit snake_case or camelCase corridor authoring on collapse edges, native frame runtime adapter, the hetero-fabric backend-race runtime for CPU/CUDA-style/WebGPU/WebNN/vendor-NPU/WASM lanes, emitted stability metadata including measurable queue and hetero-fabric theorem families, the derived bounded-affine queue-family `continuousHarris` observable/Lyapunov witness and generated theorem names, Lévy-Prokhorov exact-convergence, Lévy-Prokhorov geometric-decay, abstract Lévy-Prokhorov geometric-ergodicity theorem names, and renderer compatibility layer.
- [testing](./testing/README.md): canonical `@a0n/gnosis/test` surface, Bun/Vitest compatibility adapters, matcher/mocking layer, mixed TS+GG suite loader, deterministic test discovery/deduplication, duration-aware GG lane planning with persisted runtime history, and the Gnosis-first runner behind `gnode test`.
- [universe](./universe/README.md): the canonical executable universe kernel owned by Gnosis, including `LAW_KERNEL_ID`, `LawProgram`, certificate helpers, Buleyean audits, deterministic `secondsStructuralErrorceUniversalEpoch` + quasicrystalline vector-clock projection (`gnosisUniversalEpochClock`), the bounded Laplace witness/ceiling theorem surface, browser-safe deterministic digest helpers, and runtime metadata helpers, exported at `@a0n/gnosis/universe`.

## Related

- [Harnesses](../harnesses/README.md): subprocess entrypoints and framework-compatibility shims that the polyglot runtime calls outside the in-process Gnosis engine.

## Certified App Envelope

Gnosis now owns the canonical certified app identity surface:

- `CertifiedAppEnvelope` binds app identity, source digest, compiled artifact identity, universe certificate/digest, capability summary, dependency certificate digests, and runtime target.
- `QDoc` is the source of truth through `storeCertifiedAppEnvelopeInQDoc(...)` and `loadCertifiedAppEnvelopeFromQDoc(...)`.
- deterministic JSON mirrors exist only for boundary consumers such as Forge router responses, publish artifacts, and inspection surfaces.
- `gnode` and the precompiled artifact host now emit and reuse the same envelope identity on compile/register and cache hits.
